subject: Update Dockerfiles to be arm64v8 compatible
commit_message:
Update Dockerfiles to be arm64v8 compatible

VNC is not addressed in this commit

Change-Id: I00dc9cd6216fafb10294cf738a3327f5aa753ebf
Issue-ID: INT-553
Signed-off-by: William Lambert <wlambert@infosiftr.com>

entries.message:
Patch Set 1:

Hi William
Can you explain what you did here? Was this change fully tested?

Patch Set 1:

> Hi William
 > Can you explain what you did here? Was this change fully tested?

The base_sdc Dockerfiles were made to be arm and x86_64 compatible, the changes were only tested by running the containers and checking for obvious errors.
I couldn't find any tests for the images, so if there are some that the containers can be run against then that would be nice.

There were two main Dockerfiles I wasn't certain of; with Kibana and Elasticsearch I wasn't certain of how Chef was to be installed in the image, the recommended way of using omnitruck isn't arm-compatible. In the other Dockerfiles Chef was gem installed, so I'm not sure how you'd want it incorporated.

This submission is also an attempt at image-minimization for https://jira.onap.org/browse/INT-550

Patch Set 1:

(5 comments)

Was this tested with the SDC tests bvt ci-cd docker?
Was this tested with SDC OOM (K8S) deployment?

Patch Set 2:

(5 comments)

Could you point me to the tests/documentation, it looks like this is it https://gerrit.onap.org/r/gitweb?p=integration.git but I'm completely unfamiliar with this testing suite.

Patch Set 2:

Testing - there is a test docker on the SDC repository.
You can build the SDC locally and use the docker run script to load the dockers and run the test-apis-ci + ui-ci
https://wiki.onap.org/display/DW/Deploying+SDC+on+a+Linux+VM+for+Development

Patch Set 2:

Is the sdc/ci repo is missing some artifacts?

The Dockerfile tries to copy "dcae-ci-tests-*-jar-with-dependencies.jar"
But the repo doesn't have any .jar files

Patch Set 2:

So doing a maven install gives the artifacts for the sdc/ci image to build correctly, running it as-is seems to be the incorrect method.

I'll see about setting up the environment according to https://wiki.onap.org/display/DW/Deploying+SDC+on+a+Linux+VM+for+Development
This will probably take some time, given that I'm also completely unfamiliar with the ONAP environment and more so deal with Docker specifically, there's going to be some time involved for setting up an Ubuntu 14.04 vm, configuring the environment, setting up webseal, configuring Docker and the images to use the locally-built ones. And then I see something about vagrant in there too.

Patch Set 2:

(1 comment)

Please keep the dockers as slim as possible

Patch Set 2: Code-Review-1

Hi William, please fix according to Areli comments or abandon. Thanks
